assertions.text: Discount ladder, and traveller eligibility are in separate tables. Rolling window counting as a third table is desirable.
assertions.evidence: Table 1: 'Discount Type by Passenger Category', Table 2: 'Reis Discount Ladder', Table 4: 'Discount Dynamics (Rolling 30-Day Window)' — three separate tables covering each concern.

assertions.text: Children's flat 20% discount is represented — either as a row in the eligibility table or a separate note. It does not follow the ladder.
assertions.evidence: Table 1 row: 'Child single ticket | Child | Single | Flat 20%' and Table 3 row: 'Child flat discount | Child | 50 | 20% | 40'. The discount type 'Flat 20%' is explicitly distinguished from 'Reis (variable)'.

assertions.text: The discount ladder has multiple tiers derived from the 'every fifth trip' rule, with boundary values or value sets showing which ticket counts produce which discount. The maximum 40% discount is represented.
assertions.evidence: Table 2 shows tiers at trips 5, 10, 15, 20 with value sets '{1, 2, 3, 4}' and '{6, 7, 8, 9}', and '20 | 40%' with 'Maximum discount threshold'. The note states 'every fifth trip moves you one step higher' and '4 tiers (at 5, 10, 15, 20 trips)'.

assertions.text: The 30-day rolling window boundary is tested: a ticket at exactly 30 days is included, at 31 days is excluded.
assertions.evidence: Table 4 uses 'Days Active > 30' for all rows and discusses travel pattern changes over the window, but there is no row testing a ticket at exactly 30 days (included) versus 31 days (excluded). The boundary between day 30 and day 31 is never tested.

assertions.text: Eligibility uses human-readable values (yes/no or equivalent) rather than raw true/false. TypeConverter or similar mechanism mentioned.
assertions.evidence: TypeConverter is not mentioned anywhere in the response. The response contains no reference to TypeConverter, TypeConverterSources, or any similar conversion mechanism.

assertions.text: Value sets in the tier table contain exactly the values that produce the same discount. No cross-tier contamination.
assertions.evidence: Table 2 uses '{1, 2, 3, 4}' for 0% discount and '{6, 7, 8, 9}' for the tier-1 range. Each value set groups only trip counts that produce the same discount level; no set spans a tier boundary.

assertions.text: @DisplayName is present on test methods, OR method names read as clear descriptive titles when converted from camelCase/snake_case.
assertions.evidence: The response contains only markdown tables and prose — there is no Java/Kotlin code, no @DisplayName annotations, and no method names anywhere in the output.

assertions.text: @Description is present on at least the class or the tier method and provides context beyond what the table rows already express — such as the discount ladder structure, eligibility rules, or application context. Does NOT merely restate the column names or summarise what the rows show.
assertions.evidence: The response contains no Java/Kotlin code and no @Description annotation anywhere. It is entirely markdown tables and prose commentary.

assertions.text: TypeConverterSources is used for at least one human-readable conversion (yes/no, or rolling window notation).
assertions.evidence: Neither 'TypeConverterSources' nor 'TypeConverter' appears anywhere in the response.

assertions.text: Annotations appear in order: @DisplayName (if present), @Description (if present), @TableTest — not any other order.
assertions.evidence: The response contains no Java/Kotlin annotations of any kind — no @DisplayName, @Description, or @TableTest appear anywhere in the output.

assertions.text: @Description text uses a text block (triple-quoted string """) when the text is long, not string concatenation with +.
assertions.evidence: The response contains no @Description annotation and no Java/Kotlin code whatsoever, so no text block or string concatenation is present.
